💎Welcome
📋SOPs
🥽Security
♠️Gaming
🍸Front of House

We're glad you're here. This guide has everything you need to know before your first shift and as you settle in. Take your time going through it.

We're a smaller team, which means we're close. Everyone gets along and genuinely looks out for each other. If you ever have questions or need help with something, just ask. No one's going to make you feel dumb for it.

We have four roles here: Croupier, Security, Bartender, and Valet. No matter which role you were hired for, you're welcome to train in any of the others. Want to deal cards but you started on valet? Go for it. The more you're trained in, the more flexible you are on the floor, and that's a good thing for everyone.

Training sessions are posted regularly in Discord at a variety of times, so keep an eye out for one that fits your schedule. Something will come up.

Standard Operating Procedures
The basics. Read before your first shift.
4h
4 hours a month minimum
Split however you want. Resets on the 1st. Miss twice in a row and we part ways.
30
30 minute shift minimum
Clock in, commit to at least 30 minutes. Stay in your role before switching.
!
Never
Gamble while clocked in. Abuse patrons or staff. Fast travel doors to escape. Discuss payroll. Crimes on property.
Code of Conduct
DO
Wear correct uniform for your role
Stay on Casino Radio (in-city + Discord)
Match Discord name to in-city name
Follow chain of command
Only work roles you're trained in
DON'T
Clock in for less than 30 minutes
Check IDs unless handed to you
Enter locked areas without instruction
Use LifeInvader unless Senior+
Discuss payroll with anyone
Clocking On and Off
Arriving:
Enter professionally. The garage is not your playground.
Clock in and change into correct uniform
Tune into Casino Radio (in-city and Discord)
Leaving:
Clock out and disconnect from Discord radio
Leave respectfully unless staying as a guest
Employee Activity & LOA
First missed month: Management reaches out. Hit hours next month or we part ways.
Two consecutive: We part ways. No hard feelings, welcome to reapply.
Miss one, hit the next, clean slate.
LOA: Submit in Discord before you go dark. Approved LOA pauses activity.
Customer Interaction
Create a welcoming atmosphere
De-escalate first, always
Escalation: Security → Supervisor/Manager/Director → LSPD
Dress code:
Fully clothed. No helmets, goggles, masks.
Revealing clothes fine. Bikini tops and lingerie not.
Direct to wardrobe near the bar. Removal is last resort.
Blacklisting
⚠
Last resort. De-escalate. Warn. If they won't cooperate, issue it.
DurationReason
1 DayMinor offenses, dress code
3 DaysDisruptive/disrespectful
1 WeekFighting or arguing
1 MonthSexual harassment or violence
3 MonthsRepeated offenses
PermanentSupervisor+ approval required
Post in Blacklist Request with footage, description, duration
DO NOT blacklist while they're in chairs
Appeals via Casino Info channel
Chain of Command
Trainee
→
Employee
→
Senior Employee
→
Supervisor
→
Manager
→
Director
Follow the chain. Don't skip unless emergency. Interested in supervisor? Reach out to management.
Disciplinary Procedure
All new employees: two week probationary period.
Verbal Warning
Not on record
Written Warning
Recorded. Three = dismissal risk
Suspension
Serious offenses or pending review
Dismissal
Gross misconduct or repeated issues
Don't have to go in order. Situation determines response.
Criminal Activity & Resignation
Criminal Activity:
Criminal background for RP? Fine. No crimes clocked in, on property, or disrupting business.
Resignation:
Tell management. Stay professional on the way out.
Before You Clock In
Keep people safe, keep things calm, keep it professional, and have fun.
Tune into Casino Radio (in-city + Discord). Name must match.
Coordinate with other security. Spread out, don't cluster.
Know your zones. Gaming floor first, front door, hotspots.
On the Floor
Stay visible and alert
Interact with guests, stay aware
Never AFK. Clock out if you need time.
De-escalation
Slow down
→
Stay calm
→
Warn
→
One on one
→
Higher up / LSPD
→
Blacklist
Don't match their energy. Get them alone if heated.
Dress Code
Helmets, goggles, masks, naked: not permitted
Ask politely first
Direct to wardrobe near bar
Prescription goggles aren't a thing. Glasses in wardrobe.
Substances
Legal (alcohol, tobacco, medical marijuana): fine
Overly intoxicated: escort outside calmly
Illegal: don't confront. Text LSPD, observe from distance.
Physical Violence
!
You do not fight patrons. Period. No weapons, no threats, no chasing outside.
Self defense: last resort only
Weapon visible / life at risk: retreat, LSPD immediately
Never fast travel doors to escape. City rule break.
ID Checks
Only if handed willingly
Never snatch or force. Powergaming = ban.
RP it: "Random checks tonight, mind helping us out?"
Events
Check Discord announcements before every event
Penthouse: check every ID, one guest at a time
Car meets: not without management approval
Valet area: only if floor is covered and cross trained
Floor Map
Gaming floor = priority. Communicate positions on radio. Only security clocked in? No reason to be outside.
Table Etiquette
Make the floor exciting and welcoming. Guide the experience.
💡
Mechanics are automated. Your job: know the flow, keep commentary going, make people want to stay.
DO
Welcome arrivals, thank departures
Encourage bystanders to join
Explain stakes and how it works
Announce last hand when leaving
DON'T
Ask patrons to give up seats
Play favorites or give advice
Go AFK at tables
Step in mid-hand without asking
Best BJ view
Left Alt
First person
V
Table talk
Flex H icon
Too bright?
PostFX → Normal
Social Responsibility
Normal: plays for fun, calm, bets within means
Problem: joyless, chasing losses, emotional, beyond means
Patron mentions gambling problem? VSE option. Never bring it up first.
Concerned? Report to management, don't confront.
BlackjackTable: Sandra
DEALER
PLAYER
Practice dealing
Hands: 0
Soft Hand
Ace counted as 11. Can't bust on next hit.
Ace + 6 = Soft 17
Hard Hand
No Ace, or Ace forced to 1. Total is fixed.
10 + 7 = Hard 17
CARD VALUES
2-10
Face value
J, Q, K
10
Ace
1 or 11
PAYOUTS
Win
1:1
Blackjack
3:2
FLOW
Players place bets
Announce bets closed
Deal two each, player up, one of yours up
10 or Ace showing? Check for blackjack
Call out values as you deal
PLAYER OPTIONS
Hit: another card
Stand: keep hand
Double: double bet, one more card
Split: same value, two hands
No insurance or surrender.
DEALER
Stands on all 17s including soft 17

Players place bets
Announce spin, keep commentary
Wave hand, no more bets
Call result: number and color
Acknowledge winners/losses

HAND RANKINGS
Straight Flush
Same suit, sequence
Three of a Kind
Three same rank
Straight
Sequence, mixed suits
Flush
Same suit, not sequence
Pair
Two same rank
High Card
Highest card
Q
Qualifying: Queen-high+. Pair or better auto-qualifies. No qualify = push.
THE TWO BETS
Ante & Play: vs dealer
Pair Plus: your hand only, pays regardless
FLOW
Place Ante, Pair Plus, or both
Deal 3 face down each
Fold (forfeit Ante) or Play (match Ante)
Flip dealer cards

Front of House
Bartenders and Valets. First and last impression.
💡
Polite, calm, professional. Full attention. No side chatter mid-interaction. Represent the casino on property.
Bartending
Serve promptly and professionally
Keep bar clean and stocked
Clear glasses, walk the floor if quiet
Employee drinks free. Play the motions.
Don't just grab it. Visually make it. Emotes and /me's.
/e mechanic
Mixing
/e mechanic3
Fridge
/e medic
Fridge
/e clean
Cleaning
/e notepad
Floor orders
Valet
Greet and thank on arrival/departure
Park safely in the garage
Keep area clear, direct traffic
Remove abandoned vehicles
Valid LS driver's license required
Tell patrons how valet works
Drive smooth. No reckless.
Locked/destroyed locals: text DW Customs or East
Valet Area Rules
!
No drifting, racing, violence, drug deals, gang activity
Illegal activity: text LSPD, retreat inside
Report to Security or Management
